prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(discover_node producer_node_1)
(discover_node consumer_node_1)
(discover_node component_1)
(discover_node component_2)
(assign_executor_to_node producer_node_1 executor_slot_1)
(assign_executor_to_node consumer_node_1 executor_slot_2)
(assign_executor_to_node component_1 executor_slot_3)
(acquire_resource_for_node producer_node_1 resource_token_1)
(mark_node_ready producer_node_1 resource_token_1)
(producer_publish_data_on_channel_a producer_node_1 channel_a_1 data_item_1)
(producer_finalize_channel_a_advertisement producer_node_1 channel_a_1 resource_token_1 data_item_1)
(release_resource_from_node producer_node_1 resource_token_1)
(acquire_resource_for_node consumer_node_1 resource_token_1)
(mark_node_ready consumer_node_1 resource_token_1)
(consumer_publish_data_on_channel_b consumer_node_1 channel_b_1 data_item_1)
(consumer_finalize_channel_b_advertisement consumer_node_1 channel_b_1 resource_token_1 data_item_1)
(allocate_result_container producer_node_1 consumer_node_1 channel_a_1 channel_b_1 result_container_1)
(release_resource_from_node consumer_node_1 resource_token_1)
(acquire_resource_for_node producer_node_1 resource_token_1)
(initialize_container_for_production result_container_1 producer_node_1 resource_token_1)
(release_resource_from_node producer_node_1 resource_token_1)
(acquire_resource_for_node component_1 resource_token_1)
(mark_node_ready component_1 resource_token_1)
(attach_artifact_to_component component_1 artifact_1 result_container_1)
(prepare_component_for_evaluation component_1 artifact_1 result_container_1 resource_token_1)
(attach_special_resource_to_component component_1 special_resource_1)
(bind_dependency_handler_to_node component_1 dependency_handler_1)
(attach_auth_token_to_component component_1 auth_token_1)
(start_component_evaluation_with_special_resource component_1 special_resource_1 dependency_handler_1 artifact_1 result_container_1)
(promote_component_evaluation component_1 auth_token_1 artifact_1 result_container_1)
(finalize_component_evaluation component_1)
(attach_allocation_label_to_node component_1 allocation_label_1 resource_token_1)
(release_resource_from_node component_1 resource_token_1)
(detach_special_resource_from_component component_1 special_resource_1)
(detach_auth_token_from_component component_1 auth_token_1)
(unbind_dependency_handler_from_node component_1 dependency_handler_1)
(finalize_component_allocation component_1 executor_slot_3 allocation_label_1)
(assign_executor_to_node component_2 executor_slot_3)
(acquire_resource_for_node component_2 resource_token_1)
(mark_node_ready component_2 resource_token_1)
(attach_artifact_to_component component_2 artifact_2 result_container_1)
(prepare_component_for_evaluation component_2 artifact_2 result_container_1 resource_token_1)
(attach_special_resource_to_component component_2 special_resource_1)
(bind_dependency_handler_to_node component_2 dependency_handler_1)
(attach_auth_token_to_component component_2 auth_token_1)
(start_component_evaluation_with_special_resource component_2 special_resource_1 dependency_handler_1 artifact_2 result_container_1)
(promote_component_evaluation component_2 auth_token_1 artifact_2 result_container_1)
(finalize_component_evaluation component_2)
(attach_allocation_label_to_node component_2 allocation_label_1 resource_token_1)
(release_resource_from_node component_2 resource_token_1)
(detach_special_resource_from_component component_2 special_resource_1)
(detach_auth_token_from_component component_2 auth_token_1)
(unbind_dependency_handler_from_node component_2 dependency_handler_1)
(finalize_component_allocation component_2 executor_slot_3 allocation_label_1)
(commit_producer_result producer_node_1 result_container_1)
(commit_consumer_result consumer_node_1 result_container_1)
(acquire_resource_for_node producer_node_1 resource_token_1)
(attach_allocation_label_to_node producer_node_1 allocation_label_1 resource_token_1)
(finalize_producer_node_allocation producer_node_1 executor_slot_1 allocation_label_1)
(release_resource_from_node producer_node_1 resource_token_1)
(acquire_resource_for_node consumer_node_1 resource_token_1)
(attach_allocation_label_to_node consumer_node_1 allocation_label_1 resource_token_1)
(finalize_consumer_node_allocation consumer_node_1 executor_slot_2 allocation_label_1)
(release_resource_from_node consumer_node_1 resource_token_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem involves a dependency graph evaluation with producer_node_1, consumer_node_1, and two computation components: component_1 and component_2, both linked to the same producer and consumer, assigned to the same result_container_1.


## Examining Dependency Graph  
- The problem includes a dependency graph with producer_node_1 and consumer_node_1.  
- Two computation components, component_1 and component_2, connect to the same producer and consumer.  
- Both components are linked to the same result_container_1 for output.  
- This setup suggests evaluating how data flows through the graph and impacts computation.

## Examining Dependency Graph  
- Policy_token_2 may not be available for component_2, potentially blocking reserve_policy_for_component.  
- The reserve_policy_for_component process requires policy_token_available and component_bound_policy, but policy_token_2 is limited.  

## Exploring Component Finalization  
- Components can finalize via multiple paths: finalize_component_evaluation, complete_component_evaluation, or finalize_after_policy, all leading to entity_output_ready.  
- Finalizing requires entity_output_ready, then attach_allocation_label_to_node for committed status, followed by finalize_component_allocation to complete.  

## Investigating Resource Allocation  
- Allocation_label_1 and special_resource_1 are single-use, suggesting sequential processing for components.  
- Auth_token_1 and config_option are also limited, impacting the planning system’s resource management.  

## Planning Execution Steps  
- For producer_node_1, steps include discover, assign executor, acquire resource_token_1, and mark_node_ready to reach entity_ready.  
- To achieve ready_for_container, options include advertise then confirm (requiring handler_bound) or publish then finalize, each with specific prerequisites.

## Planning Execution Steps  
- For producer_node_1, the sequence starts with discover, assign executor, acquire resource_token_1, and mark_node_ready to reach entity_ready.  
- Publishing data (producer_publish_data_on_channel_a) requires entity_ready, bound_a, and data_avail, but doesn’t need an acquired resource initially.  
- To finalize (producer_finalize_channel_a_advertisement), acquiring resource_token_1 is necessary before finalizing, ensuring ready_for_container and data availability.  
- Advertising (producer_advertise_channel_a) also requires an acquired resource and makes channel_a_ready, but publishing afterward needs careful ordering to meet buffering requirements.  
- Data_item_1 can be reused after publishing (makes not avail) and finalizing (makes avail again), supporting sequential processing without needing multiple items.  

## Managing Resource Constraints  
- Resource_token_1 and data_item_1 are single-use, necessitating sequential processing for producer and consumer actions.  
- Executors can be assigned in parallel (three available), but resource acquisition remains serial due to the single resource_token_1.  
- Releasing resources (release_resource_from_node) can occur anytime after acquisition, but many actions still require the resource to be held.  
- Handler binding, if needed, would be serial, but the current path for producer and consumer avoids handler requirements.

## Exploring Node Preparation  
- Discover and assign executors to producer_node_1 and consumer_node_1 using executor_slot_1 and executor_slot_2, respectively, as initial steps.  
- For producer_node_1, acquire resource_token_1, mark_node_ready, and publish data on channel_a_1, making data_item_1 unavailable after publishing.  

## Handling Resource Conflicts  
- Consumer_node_1 publishing requires data_item_1 available, but it’s unavailable after producer_node_1 publishes, creating a conflict.  
- Finalizing producer_node_1’s advertisement makes data_item_1 available again, but requires resource_token_1, which was released earlier.  

## Planning Component Integration  
- Components (comp1, comp2) need container allocated and initialized before processing, linking to producer and consumer readiness.  
- With only three executors, process producer and consumer first, then handle components sequentially as executors become available.

## Managing Executor Allocation  
- With four nodes and three executors, assign exec1 to producer_node_1, exec2 to consumer_node_1, and exec3 to component_1 initially.  
- Release exec3 after component_1 completes to reuse for component_2, ensuring sequential processing due to limited executors.  

## Handling Component Preparation  
- Components require entity_ready status before attaching artifacts, which involves acquiring resource_token_1 and marking ready.  
- Attach_artifact needs container allocated, initialized, and artifact available, making it attached and bound_to_container afterward.  

## Addressing Resource Sequencing  
- Components need resource_token_1 for both mark_ready and prepare_component_for_evaluation, requiring sequential processing after producer and consumer.  
- Special_resource_1 and handler availability (single-use) mean components must process serially, ensuring bound_special and handler_bound for stage_two.  

## Planning Component Evaluation  
- Start_component_evaluation_with_special_resource requires stage_one_ready, bound_special, and handler_bound, leading to stage_two.  
- Process component_1 fully before component_2, leveraging two artifacts and ensuring resource and executor availability.

## Finalizing Component Allocation  
- After attaching allocation label to component_1, it becomes committed and bound to allocation_label_1, using resource_token_1.  
- Finalizing allocation with executor_slot_3 completes component_1, releasing the executor and allocation label, but resource_token_1 remains acquired.  

## Managing Resource Release  
- Release resource_token_1 from component_1 after attaching the label but before finalizing allocation, as finalize_alloc doesn’t affect resource status.  
- Detach special_resource_1, auth_token_1, and unbind dependency_handler_1 from component_1 after completion to make them available for component_2.  

## Preparing for Component_2  
- Component_2 can follow the same process, using available artifact_2 and the same container, with no tags or policy changes needed.  
- Ensure handler, special resource, and auth are rebound for component_2, leveraging existing detach actions for availability.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
